elements.sent-2411-424: In summary, we have studied the SC properties of $\gamma$-BiPd by solving the anisotropic Migdal-Eliashberg equations in conjunction with {\it ab initio relativistic calculations of the electron and phonon band structures as well as EPC matrix elements.}
elements.sent-2411-425: We find that $\gamma$-BiPd possesses a complex FS, consisting of two electron pockets and one hole pocket, each characterised by distinct atomic orbitals.
elements.sent-2411-426: Importantly, we discover that the superconductivity in $\gamma$-BiPd is orbital-selective, arising from Bi $p$-orbitals, and distributed anisotropically on the FS.
elements.sent-2411-427: While our results show momentum {\bf k -dependent EPC strength $\lambda_{\bf k}$ and SC gap $\Delta_{\bf k}$ across the FS, that calculated superconducting quasiparticle density of states $N_S$ spectra exhibit a U-shaped gap and $\Delta_{\bf k}$ distribution forms a single peak, indicates a single gap spin-singlet $s$-wave superconductivity in this material.}
elements.sent-2411-428: The calculated $T_c$ is $\sim$2.0 K, agreeing in order of magnitude with the experimental value of 3.3 K in $\gamma$-BiPd thin films~\cite{Unequivocal_Chiang2023 .}

elements.sent-2411-430: The predicted EPC-enhanced Sommerfeld coefficient $\gamma_n$ of $0.141$ mJ/K$^2$cm$^3$ is close to the experimental $\gamma_n$ value ($0.119$ mJ/K$^2$cm$^3$) of the isoelectronic and isostructural Bi(Pd$_{0.5}$Pt$_{0.5}$) alloy~\cite{Sharma2024 .}
elements.sent-2411-431: Our interesting findings will stimulate further experiments on $\gamma$-BiPd, such as scanning tunneling microscopy and spectroscopy, quasiparticle interference imaging, high resolution angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy and low temperature specific heat experiments.

elements.section-2411-010: Effect of Spin-Orbit Coupling on the Electronic Band Structure
elements.figure-2411-019: \includegraphics[width=0.90\columnwidth]{BiPdFig9.eps} \caption{Band structures of $\gamma$-BiPd calculated without (blue curves) and with (red curves) the SOC included.} \label{fig:bands_nosoc_soc}
elements.sent-2411-434: Due to the presence of heavy Bi atoms in $\gamma$-BiPd, the relativistic SOC is expected to change the electronic band structure significantly.
elements.sent-2411-435: Thus, to examine the SOC effects, we have calculated the band structure of $\gamma$-BiPd both with and without including the SOC, and the results are shown in Fig. A1.
elements.sent-2411-436: It is clear from Fig. A1 that the presence of the SOC substantially alters the band structure, especially the energy bands near the $E_F$.
elements.sent-2411-437: In particular, the SOC opens a large gap of about 1.5 eV at the Dirac nodal point at $\sim$1.4 eV above the $E_F$ along the $\Gamma$-A symmetry line.
elements.sent-2411-438: This greatly pushes the lower band down to below the Fermi level, resulting in the disappearance of the large hole pocket centered at the $\Gamma$ point.
elements.sent-2411-439: Figure A1 also shows that the presence of the SOC greatly changes the band structure and FS topology near the K point in the vicinity of the $E_F$.
elements.sent-2411-440: Therefore, because of these significant effects of the SOC, we present only the results from the fully relativistic calculations in this paper.. {.}
